recent_excerpts: Security researchers uncovered 'Fast16,' a pre-Stuxnet sabotage malware that targeted high-precision calculation software in Iran, tampering with results and self-propagating.
A criminal group deployed the self-propagating CanisterWiper malware against Iranian entities, exploiting misconfigured cloud storage to spread and destroy data.
A state-directed internet blackout in Iran has surpassed 1,000 cumulative hours, marking a significant escalation in digital censorship and control tactics.
